title: poa badensis subsp. multiflora
intro: Poa badensis subsp. multiflora, also known as the multiflora bluegrass or Alpine Meadow-grass, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the grass family Poaceae, native to the mountains of Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic effects. The health benefits of Poa badensis subsp. multiflora include soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of this plant, often in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
